photo_read: Front support and radiator side area are visible; previous temporary wire/loop support path and usable holes/support faces are visible, but hole centres and offsets are not measured.
decision: Fabricate or repair a serviceable two-side radiator retention set using sound existing holes where possible.
next_action: Capture ruler photos of both radiator side flanges, current wire path, hole diameters, hole centres, lower support, and fan clearance.
notes: Keep radiator plane unless dry-fit proves movement required; do not hard-clamp radiator tank or core.

photo_read: Front support is visible, but the photos do not prove that the opposite side has complete symmetrical retention.
decision: Verify side-to-side retention before releasing the radiator bracket design.
next_action: Photograph the opposite radiator side from front, rear, top, and side with a ruler and the radiator sitting in its current plane.
notes: Do not assume the existing one visible side represents the complete mounting set.

component_or_function: Lower radiator support or lower pickup holes
photo_read: Front crossmember/support hole context is visible; isolator style, hole condition, metal thickness, and lower load path are not measured.
decision: Use or repair lower pickups only after thickness, hole shape, and rubber/isolator fit are checked.
next_action: Clean the lower support area, inspect for oval holes or thin metal, then record hole diameter, pitch, edge distance, and rubber contact area.
notes: Lower support work must not trap rust or block drain/service access.

component_or_function: Radiator, fan, belt, hose, and bonnet clearance envelope
photo_read: Fan, belt, hose, and radiator packaging are visible enough to set a design constraint; exact clearance still needs measurement.
decision: Treat current radiator plane and fan clearance as a release constraint for any bracket repair.
next_action: Measure closest fan-to-radiator clearance and upper hose relaxation before and after any bracket dry-fit.
notes: Any bracket that pulls the radiator toward the fan or stresses the upper hose fails release.

photo_read: Battery location and top crossbar/clamp are visible in engine-bay overview photos; tray base, tray feet, and underside support are not visible.
decision: Inspect and upgrade the top restraint only after the tray base support is proven strong enough.
next_action: Measure battery length, width, height, terminal orientation, top clamp path, bonnet clearance, and any existing clamp holes.
notes: Clamp must not touch terminals or rely on a weak tray edge.

component_or_function: Battery tray base, tray feet, and support legs
photo_read: Current photos identify the battery area but do not show tray underside, feet, cracks, welds, or usable lower support points.
decision: Scout before fabrication; if weak, build a tray support tied into real structure rather than flat unsupported sheet.
next_action: Capture top, side, front, rear, and underside tray photos with ruler; include existing legs, cracks, corrosion, drain path, and possible lower brace route.
notes: This is the main battery-holder evidence gap.

component_or_function: Battery-side MIDI fuse plate and master cutoff carrier pickups
photo_read: Battery location is visible and MIDI/cutoff hardware is documented, but no vehicle-side carrier pickup has been released.
decision: Design a removable carrier that uses tray support or adjacent structure, not the battery tray skin alone.
next_action: Mock up a 190 x 150 mm MIDI plate plus cutoff body near the installed battery; photograph cable lug sweep, cable exits, service access, and bonnet clearance.
notes: Keep this vehicle-side carrier separate from the existing MIDI Rev C plate package.

component_or_function: Front support auxiliary holes, tabs, and clip points
photo_read: Front support and crossmember holes/tabs are visible as context, but each point is not assigned to a proven function.
decision: Do not weld generic auxiliary tabs; assign each hole or tab to radiator, line, harness, earth, exhaust, or no-refit before work.
next_action: Photograph each front support hole/tab with a label and decide keep, repair, fabricate, remove non-baseline, or unknown.
notes: This row captures the front-support part of Akbar's estimated auxiliary bracket additions.
